steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to provide evidence that Tamm's journey was a true circumnavigation. The notes supply a clear requirement: a true circumnavigation must begin and end in the same port. Evidence means showing that Tamm's journey actually met that requirement — not just stating the requirement or asserting the conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "To be considered a true circumnavigation, a journey must begin and end in the same port, and Tamm's lasted 254 days.": States the requirement correctly, then pivots to duration (254 days), which has nothing to do with whether the same-port requirement was satisfied. The evidence is never delivered. ✗
  • "Tamm began and ended his trip in the same port (Les Sables d'Olonne, France), one of the requirements for a true circumnavigation.": Directly shows Tamm met the specific requirement (same port, named) and explicitly connects that fact to what a true circumnavigation demands. This is complete evidence. ✓
  • "Tamm is an Estonian sailor who completed a true circumnavigation that ended in Les Sables d'Olonne, France.": Simply asserts the conclusion ("completed a true circumnavigation") without showing how the requirement was met — it assumes what it is supposed to prove. ✗
  • "A true circumnavigation, Tamm's journey also passed the three great capes of the Southern Ocean.": Introduces the capes detail, which appears in the notes but is not cited as a requirement for a true circumnavigation. Irrelevant to proving the same-port requirement. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
